expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: All 6 rounds are present: 'Round 1: Architect Proposes', 'Round 2: Operator Challenges', 'Round 3: Architect Defends', 'Round 4: Operator Stress-Tests', 'Round 5: Architect Final Stand', 'Round 6: CTO Decides'

expectations.text: Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Architect appears in Rounds 1, 3, 5; Operator appears in Rounds 2, 4; CTO appears in Round 6. Each has a distinct role: Architect proposes and defends, Operator challenges and stress-tests, CTO renders verdict.

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized (reliability, complexity, migration-risk, team-capability, cost, dependency, security)
expectations.evidence: All 7 challenges have categories: #1 [reliability], #2 [reliability], #3 [team-capability], #4 [reliability], #5 [reliability], #6 [complexity], #7 [complexity]. Categories are drawn from the valid set.

expectations.text: Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2 challenges
expectations.evidence: Round 4 directly references Round 3 mitigations: #1 references 'dedicated Puma instances' from Round 3 defense #1; #2 references the pivot from CRDT to OT proposed in Round 3 defense #3; #3 attacks the 'on_worker_shutdown' flush from Round 3 defense #5; #4 attacks the client reconnection strategy from Round 3 defense #4.

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts: proceed, proceed-with-conditions, prototype-first, defer, reject
Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in (challengesRaised, stressTestsRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesConceded, mitigationsHeld, mitigationsBroken, proposalModified, confidenceScore)
expectations.evidence: Debate Metrics table contains all 8 metrics: challengesRaised=7, stressTestsRaised=4, challengesSurvived=2, challengesConceded=5, mitigationsHeld=3, mitigationsBroken=2, proposalModified=yes, confidenceScore=7

expectations.text: Concrete action items with who/what/when structure
expectations.evidence: 8 action items in Round 6, each with who (Backend lead, Ops/SRE, Backend team, Frontend engineer, CTO), what (specific task), and when (e.g., 'by end of week 2', 'weeks 3-6', 'week 8')

expectations.text: Round 5 either modifies the original proposal or explicitly justifies keeping it unchanged
expectations.evidence: Round 5 includes an explicit 'Proposal modifications after two rounds of challenge' section listing 8 concrete modifications: dedicated Puma instances, Redis Stream WAL, connection admission control, operation batching, editor cap, exponential backoff, revised spike scope, and tighter timeline.

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback/escape-hatch plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: Round 5 includes explicit 'Fallback plan' sections for each challenge: blue-green deploys for reconnection storms, Rust native extension for OT latency, client operation buffer for Redis Stream failure, and feature kill switch for Redis cascading failure.
